items.name: NL Industries, Inc. (Harold Simmons / Contran)
items.thesis: Buy 87% of CompX (CIX) plus 30%+ of Kronos Worldwide (KRO) for substantially less than the look-through value -- Contran/Simmons family three-tier discount.
items.why: Triple-decker holding company structure (Contran -> Valhi VHI -> NL -> CIX/KRO); illiquid (avg vol <100k shares); legacy lead paint litigation overhang (largely settled but residuals); heavy family control (~87% by Contran).
items.edge: Pure look-through math: NL mcap $290m vs $470m+ of public market value of stakes alone, plus net cash. Trades at 0.86x P/B and 5x P/E. Catalyst-light but priced for permanent discount; pays out cash via dividends and special distributions.
catalysts: KRO TiO2 cycle inflection (current TiO2 prices near multi-year lows)

items.name: Galapagos NV
items.thesis: Belgian biotech with ~$54/sh in net cash and TYK2 + CAR-T pipeline thrown in for free -- spinoff abandoned but capital return is real.
items.why: Cell therapy program is being wound down (May 2025 abandoned spinoff); market is pricing in cash burn; no near-term commercial product; biotech sentiment weak; foreign listing (Euronext + Nasdaq ADR) deters US holders.
items.edge: Negative enterprise value: $30 stock vs $54 cash means market is paying you to take the pipeline. TYK2 Phase 3 readout early 2026 = binary catalyst. Even partial credit for cell therapy IP sale (post wind-down) is upside. New mgmt has signaled appetite for capital return.
catalysts: Capital return / tender or large buyback

items.name: HUYA Inc. (Tencent-controlled Chinese game streamer)
items.thesis: Trades essentially at net cash -- Tencent owns 67% (95% voting) and pays special dividends; live-streaming business is a free option.
items.why: China ADR / VIE structure risk; Chinese gaming regulatory overhang; Tencent control creates take-private fear at unfair price; revenue declining low-double-digit y/y; small float (only ~33% public).
items.edge: Negative EV after subtracting net cash; HUYA paid a $0.66/ADS special dividend in 2024 plus ongoing distributions. If Tencent ever takes private, the floor is net cash. Already declared further capital return programs. Pure 'heads I win, tails I don't lose much' setup.
catalysts: Tencent take-private (history of doing this with controlled subs)

items.name: Madison Square Garden Sports Corp. (Dolan family)
items.thesis: Buy 100% of NY Knicks + NY Rangers for ~$8B mcap when last private comps imply $11-13B fair value; February 2026 board approval to explore minority sale could finally crystallize the 'Dolan discount.'
items.why: Persistent Dolan/family-control 'discount'; sports franchises trade infrequently in public markets; lack of dividend; CBA uncertainty; lack of clear monetization path historically. James Dolan's contentious public profile.
items.edge: Board unanimously approved exploring possible spin-off or minority stake sale in February 2026 -- first concrete catalyst to mark assets to market. Recent NBA franchise comps (Celtics sold at $6.1B in 2025; Lakers at $10B in 2025) and NHL franchise comps (Senators $1.3B) pressure the discount. Citi and JPMorgan target $370+ in early 2026.
catalysts: Minority stake sale of Knicks or Rangers
New NBA media rights deal flow-through

items.name: News Corporation (Murdoch family - Class A)
items.thesis: 61% stake in REA Group alone is worth ~$9.2B -- buy News Corp at $14.4B mcap and get Dow Jones, HarperCollins, News UK and Foxtel for free.
items.why: Persistent Murdoch dual-class control discount (NWS B-shares non-voting); complexity across geographies and businesses; legacy print perception drags despite being mostly digital now; FX exposure to AUD; family succession finalized but consolidation/spin not yet announced.
items.edge: Look-through math: 61% of REA's $15B mcap = $9.2B + Dow Jones at modest 15x EBITDA = $10B+ + HarperCollins ~$2.5B + News UK ~$1.5B + cash $2B. Even with central costs, fair value $20-22B vs $14.4B. Pending Murdoch trust resolution post-Lachlan succession may unlock simplification. Average analyst PT $34.43.
catalysts: Possible Dow Jones spin or carve-out
Murdoch family settlement reduces governance overhang

items.name: Reinet Investments S.C.A. (Johann Rupert vehicle)
items.thesis: Post-PIC and BAT sales, ~80% of Reinet's NAV is now cash; trades at ~35% discount to liquid NAV with Rupert family weighing capital return.
items.why: Luxembourg-domiciled, listed primarily on JSE/Lux/Euronext Amsterdam (US ADR REINY thinly traded); Rupert family majority control; capital allocation uncertainty -- market fears NAV-destructive M&A; persistent holdco discount across European structures.
items.edge: Rare 'event already happened' setup: PIC sale closed March 2026; cash now visible on the balance sheet. Hard to argue for >20% discount on a portfolio that's overwhelmingly cash. Likely capital return (special dividend or large buyback). Discount has narrowed from 50%+ historically toward 30-35%; further compression likely.
catalysts: Special dividend or large buyback post-PIC close

items.name: Bolloré SE (Vincent Bolloré family)
items.thesis: Buy Bolloré at ~40% discount to NAV; embedded discount-on-discount on Vivendi/UMG plus a free option on the May 2026 Court of Cassation Vivendi tender ruling.
items.why: Triple-decker holding structure (Bolloré -> Compagnie de l'Odet -> Vivendi -> UMG); family control overhang (>70% via Odet); recent Vivendi spin restructure perceived as unfair to minorities; persistent French holdco discount; complex cross-shareholdings.
items.edge: AGT and other holdco specialists call this the cheapest blue-chip holdco in Europe. NAV discount at ~40% vs 26% historical average. Pershing Square's April 2026 $60B UMG bid (and proposed NYSE listing) crystallizes UMG value at premium. Court of Cassation hearing on Vivendi tender May 22, 2026 -- if Bolloré loses, mandatory tender at fair value re-rates Vivendi stake.

items.name: Prosus N.V. (Naspers tech holding)
items.thesis: Persistent ~38% discount to NAV; Prosus is sustained-buyback machine using Tencent share sales to retire its own equity below intrinsic, mechanically re-rating NAV per share.
items.why: Holdco discount endemic to European-listed structures with concentrated single asset; perceived China/Tencent regulatory risk; complex e-commerce assets harder to value; low free float despite $200B mcap; KraneShares/Tencent ETF substitutes available.
items.edge: Open-ended 'Tencent-funded buyback' is the most mathematically powerful discount-narrowing engine in any large-cap holdco. From 2022 launch through early 2024, holdco discount narrowed 21 pts unlocking $30B and grew NAV/share 8%. With $200B mcap and 38% discount, every dollar of buyback compounds. Naspers has launched a similar program of its own.

items.name: Groupe Bruxelles Lambert (Frère/Desmarais families)
items.thesis: Belgian Frère/Desmarais holdco at ~30%+ discount to NAV with active portfolio rotation (EUR 1.7B sold to Carlyle AlpInvest at 9% blended discount in late 2025/early 2026) and ongoing buyback.
items.why: Classic family-controlled European holdco discount (Frère/Desmarais via Pargesa-equivalent now consolidated); cyclical exposure (Adidas, Holcim, Pernod premium spirits weakness); private bucket harder to value; legacy 'sleepy' image despite recent activity.
items.edge: GBL has accelerated portfolio simplification: divested EUR 1.7B of NAV in late 2025 to Carlyle AlpInvest at only 9% discount (vs. GBL's own 31% discount) -- accretive arbitrage. Aggressive buyback program. New CEO (Sophie Lambin from 2024) more shareholder-focused. NAV per share EUR 113 at year-end 2024.

items.name: Sofina S.A. (Boël family Belgian holdco)
items.thesis: Boël family Belgian holdco with 50+ year track record; ~25% discount to NAV after monster widening from 6% mid-2025 -- valued at ~0.75x of a high-quality private-and-listed VC/PE portfolio.
items.why: Discount widened from 6.1% mid-2025 to 17.4% end-2025, then further to ~25% by April 2026. Reasons: Byju's writedown overhang, China internet exposure (ByteDance indirect), private valuation skepticism, Belgium small-cap illiquidity, family-control overhang.
items.edge: 30+ year compound NAV growth ~13% annually. Discount near multi-year wides offers asymmetric entry. Active buyback below NAV. Several portfolio names (Stripe, ByteDance) at potential 2026-2027 IPO catalysts. Trades roughly EUR 22 cents on the euro for late-stage VC at quality manager.

items.name: Compass Diversified Holdings (Berkshire-style mid-market)
items.thesis: Mid-market Berkshire-style holdco crushed in 2025 on accounting issues at Lugano subsidiary; trades at deep discount to SOTP of 10 niche-leader subsidiaries even after writedowns.
items.why: Lugano accounting irregularities discovered 2025 forced restatement and CEO replacement; SEC investigation overhang; high distribution previously cut; significant debt at HoldCo (~$1.8B); K-1 partnership previously (now C-corp); market down 116% YoY by April 2026 from absolute lows but still suppressed.
items.edge: Even excluding Lugano entirely, remaining segments generate ~$330m EBITDA which at 8x = $2.6B EV minus net debt = equity ~$800m+ vs current $781m mcap. New CEO and refreshed board began 2026 turnaround. BOA + 5.11 + Marucci alone justify mcap. Insider buying. Distressed asset sale (Velocity already announced) further crystallizes value.
catalysts: Resolution of Lugano restatement and SEC inquiry

items.name: Boston Omaha Corporation (Buffett grandnephew)
items.thesis: Adam Peterson (Buffett's grand-nephew) holdco trading at ~30% discount to tangible book; cash-generative billboard + broadband + insurance + asset management.
items.why: Co-CEO departure (Alex Rozek left April 2024) raised governance concerns; Sky Harbour stake mark-down volatility; market frustrated with 'too slow' capital deployment; small cap illiquidity (~$1m daily volume); some 'value trap' perception per March 2026 Motley Fool piece.
items.edge: Tangible book ~$18/sh after Sky Harbour appreciation; trades $12.53. Operating segments combined produce ~$50m EBITDA, valued conservatively at 10x = $500m equity vs $388m mcap. New buyback authorized. Median analyst PT $18 implies 44% upside. Closely-held by Peterson (Magnolia Capital).
catalysts: Boston Omaha Asset Management AUM growth (third-party fees)

items.name: SiriusPoint Ltd. (Dan Loeb / Third Point reinsurer)
items.thesis: Trades at ~0.74x book despite 23.6% diluted-BVPS growth in 2025; CM Bermuda buyback closed and Loeb retains 9.5%, having accumulated through 2026 on standstill -- floor now visible.
items.why: Persistent 0.7-0.8x book trading post-Loeb take-private discussions stalled (Feb 2024 standstill, refreshed in 2025); reinsurance pricing softening; tail risk from legacy 2018-2020 underwriting overhang; Third Point investment portfolio volatility; 'orphaned' name without consistent equity sponsorship.
items.edge: BVPS compounded 28% in 2025; share repurchases below book (2m share program announced); Loeb still owns 9.5% having sold via secondary at ~$15. Stock at $20 vs BVPS $19.40 ex-AOCI suggests under 1x even after rally. Path to 1x book = 35% upside. Continued accretion from buybacks.

items.name: Greenlight Capital Re Ltd. (David Einhorn vehicle)
items.thesis: Trades at 0.73x book; BVPS grew 13.8% in 2025 (8.1% in Q4 alone) above cost of equity; Einhorn-managed investment portfolio plus underwriting turnaround.
items.why: Persistent 'Einhorn discount' due to long/short portfolio volatility (now derisked); legacy underwriting from 2018-2020 capacity; small float (~$480m mcap); Cayman-domicile insurance reduces institutional ownership; investor skepticism after years of below-book trading.
items.edge: Q4 2025 BVPS grew 8.1% in single quarter; full-year 13.8% above cost of capital; share buybacks continuing at $14 levels (well below book $20.43). Innovations Unit minority equity stakes (Hippo, Cytora, Galileo) periodically marked up. Discount has held but BVPS growth means absolute upside compounds.

items.name: Tejon Ranch Co. (270,000-acre California land bank)
items.thesis: 270,000 contiguous acres 60 mi north of LA with entitlements for 35,000+ homes and 35M sqft commercial; market gives the land bank a tiny haircut despite Hard Rock Casino opening 2026.
items.why: Slow monetization perceived; Centennial entitlement litigation reset timeline; California land politics; persistent 'value trap' label; lack of dividend at scale; small cap (~$510m); active activist (Bulldog) driving change but uncertain outcome.
items.edge: Just 270,000 contiguous CA acres alone at $4-5k/acre conservative = $1B+; entitlement-ready acres command 10-20x. Hard Rock Casino opening 2026 demonstrates monetization path. Terra Vista lease-up demonstrates execution. Bulldog Investors activist board engagement; June 2025 board concluded strategic review with new direction. Trades at 0.75x book.
catalysts: Continued Tejon Ranch Commerce Center build-to-suit deals
Possible take-private by infrastructure/real estate investor

items.name: ITOCHU Corporation (Japanese sogo shosha) -- ADR
items.thesis: Highest-quality Japanese trading house at ~25-30% discount to fair-value SOTP per Morningstar; 23-36% upside to fair value; non-resources tilt yields most stable earnings of the five sogo shoshas Buffett bought.
items.why: Berkshire owns 10.23% but discount persists vs. Western peers; Japan listing penalty for US investors; FX exposure (JPY/USD); 'sogo shosha' opacity; resource cycle exposure even though under-indexed; market underweights consumer/retail (FamilyMart) value vs commodity peers.
items.edge: Morningstar fair value implies 23-36% upside across all five trading houses, with ITOCHU best-in-class for ROE and consumer/non-resources exposure. Buffett still buying. Aggressive buybacks (1.4T yen authorized 2024-25). FamilyMart take-private (2020) was first SOTP catalyst; further consumer carve-outs possible. ADR (ITOCY) avoids Japan-direct settlement complexity.

items.name: Mitsubishi Corporation (Buffett's largest sogo shosha)
items.thesis: Largest Japanese sogo shosha; Berkshire just lifted to 10.23%; trades at modest premium to book but ~25% discount to Morningstar fair value SOTP; mega-deal Aethon shale acquisition demonstrates capital deployment.
items.why: Resource exposure perceived as cyclical (commodity prices); Japan listing premium not yet fully closed despite Buffett halo; sogo shosha business model opaque to Western analysts; FX (JPY) drag; regulatory environment in resource-heavy regions.
items.edge: Berkshire 10.23% stake (and growing) provides validation. Largest, most diversified, lowest-cost-of-capital sogo shosha. Aethon shale deal expands US exposure. TSE 'PBR<1' campaign and management mid-term plan (MC Shared Value 2024) commit to 9-10% ROE and aggressive buybacks. ADR (MSBHF) tradeable for US investors despite low daily volume; main listing 8058.T highly liquid.

items.name: Power Corporation of Canada (Desmarais family)
items.thesis: ~20% discount to NAV; controls Great-West Life (66.9%), IGM Financial (62.1%), Wealthsimple (83.6%), plus stakes in GBL/Pargesa and alt-asset platforms.
items.why: Triple-decker historical structure (since simplified 2020); Desmarais family controls via multiple voting shares; Great-West Life conservative valuation drag; private/illiquid sleeves (Wealthsimple); persistent Canadian holdco discount.
items.edge: Wealthsimple is the hidden gem -- Canada's leading digital broker/wealth manager, profitable 2024, valuations of comparable fintechs (Robinhood ~25x earnings) imply C$12-15B+ value, captured at conservative book in NAV. New CEO (Jeffrey Orr through 2024) shareholder-aligned. Aggressive buybacks. Discount has narrowed from 30% historical to 20% but room for further compression with Wealthsimple monetization.

items.name: Onex Corporation (Gerry Schwartz vehicle)
items.thesis: Canadian PE holdco at ~30% discount to NAV; management openly says discount 'makes no sense' and is buying back stock aggressively; $50B+ AUM platform.
items.why: Canadian holdco discount; PE valuation skepticism in elevated rate environment; Gluskin Sheff write-downs in past; complex Schwartz multiple-voting shares (still founder-controlled though Schwartz transitioning); slow-realizations narrative.
items.edge: $8B realizations in 2025 ($800m to Onex) demonstrates exit machine working. Management explicitly identifies 30-40 point discount as anomalous; aggressive buyback commitments. Gerry Schwartz stepping back to chairman (succession to Bobby Le Blanc as CEO 2024); cleaner story. Convex Insurance IPO/sale potential = major catalyst. ADR (ONEXF) thin but Canadian listing liquid.

items.name: Seaboard Corporation (Bresky family)
items.thesis: Bresky-controlled diversified ag/transport/energy/sugar conglomerate at largest discount-to-book since the 1980s; 14% interest in Butterball, full ownership of Seaboard Marine, Seaboard Foods (#3 US pork), Seaboard Overseas Trading.
items.why: Pork/protein cycle severely depressed 2023-2025; sugar/ethanol cyclically weak; Bresky family >70% control limits institutional ownership; tiny float; minimal sell-side coverage (~zero analyst); $2,350+ share price ($2k+ per-share) deters retail; complexity across 6 segments.
items.edge: Trades at deepest discount to underlying assets in 40 years per Seeking Alpha analysis. Each segment in cyclical trough simultaneously -- statistically rare. Book value ~$4,500/share with conservative marks. Massive cash and securities portfolio at HoldCo (~$700m). Pork prices began recovering Q4 2025-Q1 2026. Patient money: Bresky family rarely sells but buybacks have happened at depressed levels.

items.name: Dundee Corporation (Goodman family mining holdco)
items.thesis: Goodman family holdco trades at ~50-65% discount to NAV; CEO Jonathan Goodman pivoting toward 'scalable mining platform' with several recent monetizations.
items.why: Tiny float (~C$95m mcap); historic value destruction under previous management (Ned Goodman era); illiquid junior mining valuation skepticism; Canadian small-cap discount; Goodman family multiple-voting shares limit governance reforms; some assets hard-to-value private.
items.edge: Jonathan Goodman (son of founder) executing turnaround; multiple 2025-2026 monetizations crystallized cash. New Found Gold unit sale + Borborema royalty sale = $200m+ cash in 12 months for $95m mcap company. Even at deep discount, mathematics favor patience. 'Mining platform' strategy may attract third-party LP capital. Highly asymmetric.
catalysts: Possible reorganization or Goodman family take-private

items.name: Westaim Corporation (HG Vora-backed financial services holdco)

items.why: TSX Venture-listed (TSXV) reduces institutional ownership; Skyward stake being sold over time creates overhang; Arena private credit valuation opaque; Canadian holdco discount; small float; specialized insurance/credit hard for generalists; HG Vora (Parag Vora) involvement opaque.
items.edge: Skyward Specialty up 70%+ since IPO -- residual stake worth substantial portion of Westaim's mcap by itself. Arena private credit platform scaling AUM (~$3B+ AUM). Westaim has executed secondary tranches and used proceeds for buybacks. Discount narrows mechanically as Skyward stake monetizes and cash returned.
catalysts: Continued Skyward Specialty share sales at higher prices

items.name: Liberty Media Corp - Formula One Group (Series C)

items.why: Tracker complexity (Liberty Media tracker structure); MotoGP integration risk (just-completed Nov 2024 deal); Wells Fargo cut PT to $89 from $95 in April 2026 on race calendar concerns; Andretti/Cadillac entry pricing; Las Vegas GP attendance fluctuations; 'Drive to Survive' fade narrative.
items.edge: F1 alone valued at $20-22B by sell-side comps (vs Liberty paid $4.4B in 2017). MotoGP brings synergies + global scale. Liberty Media has stated intent to collapse tracker into operating company eventually (track record: did it with SiriusXM, Live, etc.). Goldman maintains Buy. Discount typical post-major M&A; narrows in 12-18 months.
catalysts: Possible roll-up of F1 into operating co (eliminating tracker)

post_bankruptcy.title: F. POST-BANKRUPTCY EMERGENCES
post_bankruptcy.blurb: Companies emerged from Ch 11 in last 24mo

items.name: WW International, Inc. (WeightWatchers)
items.thesis: Clean balance sheet plus pivot to clinical/GLP-1 weight management gives WW a second life with a tiny float and creditor-shareholders motivated to monetize after lockups expire.
items.why: Former noteholders (Goldman, Permira, Atlas Merchant, etc.) own ~91% of new equity and want cash, not a turnaround story; selling pressure plus headline taint from second BK keeps multiple compressed.
items.edge: Sub-$120M market cap on a brand with $750M+ revenue and a real GLP-1 telehealth business (Sequence acquisition) — basically a free option on clinical pivot working.
catalysts: First post-emergence quarterly print showing clinical revenue inflection
Lock-up expirations and creditor distribution complete in 2026 (clears overhang)
Potential strategic acquirer (Pharma/GLP-1 manufacturer) for clinical channel

items.name: Vroom, Inc.
items.thesis: Wound down used-car ecommerce, now a pure subprime auto-loan run-off (UACC) and AI/data play (CarStory) with massive NOLs and creditor float that will rotate over time.
items.why: Five million share float is invisible to institutions; creditor-shareholders (Mudrick, Soros, etc.) face restricted-stock distribution mechanics; no sell-side coverage; market thinks it is a melting ice cube.
items.edge: Trades below tangible book; large NOLs (~$1.5B) shielded under Section 382 thanks to BK; CarStory AI assets undervalued; UACC run-off generates positive cash flow.
catalysts: Sale or strategic monetization of CarStory data/AI
S-1 registration of creditor shares clearing technical overhang

items.name: Wolfspeed, Inc.
items.thesis: Reorganized silicon-carbide pure-play with right-sized capex, clean balance sheet, $2B in CHIPS Act funding intact, and a tight float ahead of the SiC EV adoption inflection in 2026-2028.
items.why: Spectacular post-emergence pop (1,700% intraday) followed by creditor-shareholder selling; equity holders are hedge funds (Apollo, others) who have to mark distributions and lighten over the next 12-18 months.
items.edge: Only US-domiciled SiC pure-play; Mohawk Valley fab is operational; clean cap table eliminates the death-spiral thesis; CHIPS funding gives FCF runway.
catalysts: Sequential gross margin expansion as Mohawk Valley utilization ramps
EV OEM SiC design wins disclosed
Russell index reconstitution adds passive demand

items.name: Spirit Aviation Holdings, Inc. (Spirit Airlines)
items.thesis: Equity-stub option on Spirit either restructuring out of its second BK with fleet reduction to ~100 aircraft or finding a strategic buyer (Frontier merger rumors) before liquidation.
items.why: Universal post-BK creditor-dump on first emergence was vindicated by Chapter 22; consensus prices in liquidation. If a Frontier or other ULCC merger materializes, FLYYQ could see a multiple-bagger pop on a still-tradeable stub.
items.edge: Cheap optionality at $6M market cap on a brand with national US slot portfolio at FLL/MCO/LAS plus Latin/Caribbean route authority that has scarcity value to any acquirer.
catalysts: Confirmation of second reorg plan with new equity issuance
Fuel price normalization removing liquidation pressure

items.name: Mallinckrodt plc / Keenova Therapeutics (post-Endo merger)
items.thesis: OTC-stranded merged Mallinckrodt/Endo branded pharma at micro market cap pending NYSE relisting and Par Health spin completion — pure stranded-security mispricing on $700M+ EBITDA business.
items.why: Stuck on OTC pink sheets pending exchange relisting; institutional buyers cannot own; opioid stigma and post-BK creditor distribution tax loss selling; very thin float pre-relist.
items.edge: Two consecutive bankruptcies have wiped legacy holders entirely — entire cap table is sophisticated distressed funds (Glendon, Silver Point, etc.) with cost basis near zero willing to support relisting.
catalysts: Endo merger synergy realization on quarterly prints

items.name: Par Health (spinoff from Mallinckrodt-Endo)
items.thesis: Post-emergence generics-and-injectables spin owned by distressed-debt funds with no natural buyer base — classic forced-selling spinoff stub at trough generic pricing.
items.why: Distributed to Mallinckrodt shareholders who themselves received shares through bankruptcy creditor distribution — double-removed from natural holders; no analyst coverage; trades on grey market initially.
items.edge: US-domiciled API/sterile injectable capacity has strategic value amid drug shortage policy environment; tariff tailwinds favor US manufacturing.
catalysts: Drug shortage policy actions favoring US manufacturers

items.name: Lumen Technologies, Inc.
items.thesis: Restructured legacy telco that has pivoted to AI/data center connectivity ($8.5B in new contracts including Microsoft) — distressed-debt holders rotating out as fundamentals inflect.
items.why: Tagged as a melting-ice-cube wireline incumbent; distressed funds that drove the 2024 exchange want to sell; the $5B+ in 2024 AI infrastructure wins not in numbers yet.
items.edge: Largest US fiber backbone after AT&T with key data-center adjacency; AI capex cycle creates real backlog for hyperscaler interconnect; balance sheet now lasts to 2027 maturity wall.
catalysts: Sequential growth in Lumen Digital revenue from AI contracts
Sale of consumer fiber business (Quantum) to refinance further
First full year of AI revenue recognition in 2026

items.name: Diebold Nixdorf, Incorporated
items.thesis: Best-executed post-BK ATM/POS systems incumbent (4x since emergence) — included as the proof-of-concept for the post-BK creditor-dump-then-rip pattern this screen is hunting.
items.why: Initial creditor-distribution selling pressure has now mostly cleared; included for context as a successful working example of the thesis (now arguably fairly valued at $88).
items.edge: Cleaned-up balance sheet; rationalized cost base; secular branch-automation tailwinds in EM banking.

items.name: Hertz Global Holdings, Inc.
items.thesis: Re-restructured rental-car platform where original 2021 creditor-shareholders have largely exited; current setup is a standard activist + cyclical rental-car turnaround, not a fresh post-BK overhang.
items.why: Lingering Polestar/Tesla EV write-down stigma; market still treats it as broken-thesis stock despite Wall Street veteran board.
items.edge: Pershing Square and Knighthead alignment; ABS funding flexibility for fleet rotation back to ICE.

items.name: EMERGED-PRIVATE COMPANIES (excluded from screen but verified in window)

items.edge: n/a
catalysts: Watch for any of these to file S-1 for re-IPO 2026-2027 (typical creditor exit horizon)

items.why: Saba beneficially owns 22.57% (~22.45M shares cost ~$334M). On Feb 18 2026 Saba submitted notice to nominate seven independent trustees for 2026 annual meeting. Conditional withdrawal of Lawrence Epstein nomination depends on board not exceeding 9 seats.

items.name: BlackRock Science and Technology Term Trust
items.thesis: Saba accumulated 7.94% (~5.46M shares for $93.8M) Jan-Mar 2026; fund holds 30%+ illiquid private tech, term expires 2031. Activist pressure for tender or open-end conversion.
items.why: 13D/A filed Jan 14, 2026 disclosing 7.94% stake. Saba added shares through March 13, 2026 (Amendment No. 10). Trades at persistent 8-11% discount due to private-company valuation skepticism.

items.name: BlackRock Capital Allocation Term Trust

items.name: The Mexico Fund, Inc.

items.why: Saba owned 1,691,300 shares (11.73%) as of November 30, 2025; continued buying in Dec 2025. Initial 13D July 24, 2025 explicitly cites potential liquidation. Mexico Fund acknowledged Saba investment July 2025.

items.name: ASA Gold and Precious Metals Limited
items.thesis: Saba up to ~30% stake; two directors resigned Aug 2025 with standstill. Continued accumulation Q4 2025-Q1 2026 keeps pressure on board for further governance changes.
items.why: Saba grew stake from 17.24% (Aug 2025) to 19.53% (Oct 2025) to 29.86% (Feb 2026). Two directors (Hoene, Donovan) resigned Aug 6, 2025 under standstill. Multiple 13D/A amendments. Stock has rallied with gold but discount remains ~10%.

items.name: Highland Opportunities and Income Fund (NexPoint)
items.thesis: Persistent 50%+ NAV discount; Saba initiated 13D Aug 2025, scaled to 9.4% by April 2026. NexPoint-managed fund highly contested with potential conversion catalysts.
items.why: Saba's 13D filed Aug 1, 2025 at 5.05%. Multiple amendments to 9.4% by April 10, 2026 (~$28M paid). Massive 50%+ discount reflects governance/management skepticism. NexPoint took over management from Highland.

items.name: Adams Natural Resources Fund, Inc.

items.why: Saba 13D/A discloses 8.31% stake (2,285K shares of 27.5M outstanding). Q1 2026 NAV total return 32.3% on oil rally. Discount persistent at ~15-18% despite strong performance.

items.name: BlackRock Health Sciences Term Trust
items.thesis: Saba/BlackRock settlement Jan 2025 produced 40% tender at 99.5% NAV (commenced Mar 21, 2025). Standstill expires after 2027 proxy season; residual discount is opportunity.

items.name: BlackRock Innovation and Growth Term Trust
items.thesis: BIGZ Saba/BlackRock settlement produced 50% tender at 99.5% NAV (commenced Jun 9, 2025). Fund subsequently absorbed; ticker no longer trades.

items.name: Nuveen Pennsylvania Quality Municipal Income Fund

items.name: High Income Securities Fund (formerly Putnam High Income Securities)
items.thesis: Now Bulldog-managed CEF investing in other discounted CEFs; 10%+ discount despite activist stewardship — managed distribution plan and ongoing tenders narrow gap.
items.why: Trades at -11.5% discount per Feb 2026 data, ~10.6% currently. Fund targets 10% annual distribution rate based on $6.96 NAV (12/31/25). Quarterly $0.058/share distributions for Q2 2026.
catalysts: Continued tender offers under Bulldog plan

items.name: Total Return Securities Fund (formerly Swiss Helvetia)

items.name: Lazard Global Total Return & Income Fund, Inc.

items.why: Bulldog Investors holds amended 13D position in LGI. Specific stake level current; Bulldog typically engages on board nominations and tender offers.

items.name: abrdn Global Infrastructure Income Fund

items.why: Saba 13D/A reports 1,423,673 shares = 4.92% as of March 13, 2025. Fund now trades at +6.2% premium to NAV — Saba's intervention worked. Position de-emphasized.

items.name: abrdn Total Dynamic Dividend Fund

items.name: abrdn Global Dynamic Dividend Fund

items.why: Saba 13D filed July 25, 2024. Fund now at premium; campaign objective met. Saba stake may be reduced as discount has flipped to premium.

misc.title: H. MISC SPECIAL SITUATIONS
misc.blurb: Rights offerings, SPAC liq, FDA binaries, lockups, index, litigation, self-tenders, converts

items.name: Gabelli Equity Trust
items.thesis: Closed-end equity fund running transferable rights offering at $5.00 subscription, restruck and extended; oversubscription privilege creates accretion opportunity for shareholders.
items.edge: Rights trade at a value tied to discount; shareholders who oversubscribe get NAV-accretive shares while non-subscribers face dilution. Classic CEF rights-offering game.

items.name: BlackRock Corporate High Yield Fund
items.thesis: BlackRock CEF running 1-for-5 transferable rights offering at $8.69 (90% of NAV); $283M raise creates near-term technical pressure on shares.
items.edge: Rights typically pressure underlying through the offering window; post-expiration, the fund typically tightens its discount. Oversubscribers can buy NAV at 10% discount.
catalysts: Post-offering NAV mark and distribution announcement

items.name: TCW Strategic Income Fund
items.thesis: TCW CEF transferable rights offering at $4.52 subscription price; offering closed March 18, 2026, with post-offering NAV reset and discount narrowing playable.
items.edge: Post-rights overhang lifts; CEF discounts often re-rate after offerings settle and sponsor refreshes distribution policy.
catalysts: Final share count and NAV update

items.name: BlackRock Utilities, Infrastructure & Power Opportunities Trust
items.thesis: BlackRock infrastructure CEF announced rights offering Feb 27, 2026; rights at discount to NAV create dilution but oversubscription is NAV-accretive.
items.edge: Pre-record-date overhang already absorbed; positive AI/data-center exposure means post-offering discount may compress.

items.name: Cornerstone Total Return Fund
items.thesis: Cornerstone CEF rights offering oversubscribed by $207M at higher-than-expected price; shows strong shareholder demand and NAV-accretive raise.
items.edge: Strong oversubscription signals shareholder confidence; managed-distribution policy supported by additional capital base.

items.name: Artius II Acquisition Inc
items.thesis: $228M trust SPAC with deadline Aug 14, 2026 (or Feb 14, 2027 with definitive agreement); going concern risk creates yield-to-liquidation opportunity.
items.edge: Buying near $10.25 captures yield-to-liquidation plus optionality on a deal pop. Floor at trust value.
catalysts: Potential deal announcement creating de-SPAC pop

items.name: AlphaTime Acquisition Corp
items.thesis: Small SPAC extended deadline to December 4, 2026 with eight one-month extension options; classic yield-to-trust trade.
items.edge: Small float, easy to accumulate near trust value (~$10.50), redemption right at any extension vote.
catalysts: Final liquidation Dec 2026 if no deal

items.name: Cartesian Growth Corporation II
items.thesis: Delisted SPAC trades on OTC with $37.8M trust; new August 5, 2026 deadline post-large redemptions makes near-term liquidation likely.
items.edge: OTC delisting creates orphan illiquidity discount; redemption right intact at trust value provides asymmetric payoff.

items.name: Dynamix Corporation
items.thesis: Dynamix exited Ether Machine merger, banked $50M breakup fee; liquidation deadline Nov 22, 2026 if no replacement deal.
items.edge: Trust now augmented by $50M breakup fee — pro-rata trust value above $10.35; downside protected.

items.name: A SPAC III Acquisition Corp
items.thesis: SPAC with November 12, 2026 combination deadline; trades near trust providing 4-5% YTM if liquidates.
items.edge: Standard SPAC arb - buy below trust, redeem at deadline; downside locked at trust value.

items.name: Reddit Inc
items.thesis: Reddit IPO Mar 2024; insider lockup expired Sep 2024 with shortened expiry mechanic; remaining secondary supply continues to weigh.
items.edge: Post-lockup secondary tape can create dislocation lows on heavy insider sale days; advertising revenue inflection underway.

items.name: Venture Global Inc
items.thesis: LNG exporter IPO'd Jan 2025 at $25; lockup expired July 22, 2025 triggering insider sales and ~40% drop; now busted-IPO at deep discount.
items.edge: Largest 2025 IPO trading 42% below offer; long-dated LNG offtake economics intact; capitulation overhang largely worked through.

items.name: CoreWeave Inc
items.thesis: AI infra IPO Mar 2025; Aug 17 2025 lockup unleashed $1B+ insider sales; stock dropped 46%; secondary IPOs and convert overhang remain.
items.edge: Heavy insider sale technical largely digested; AI infra demand undimmed; possible re-rating if Mag-tier hyperscaler contracts confirmed.

items.name: Astera Labs Inc
items.thesis: AI connectivity chip IPO Mar 2024; lockup long expired; secondaries cleared overhang; pure-play on PCIe Gen6/CXL ramp.
items.edge: Now a clean-float AI connectivity name; recent surge to $165 then fade to $106 reflects volatility around hyperscaler capex narratives.

items.name: Newsmax Inc
items.thesis: Newsmax IPO'd early 2025 with explosive first-day pop then collapse; insider lockup expiration in late 2025 added overhang.
items.edge: Heavily distributed; political/news cycle catalysts and small public float can drive sharp moves either direction.

items.name: Maplebear Inc (Instacart)
items.thesis: Instacart IPO'd Sep 2023; all lockups expired; recurring secondary distributions but business profitable, advertising business growing.
items.edge: Profitable platform with sticky GTV growth; ad revenue mix expansion supports multiple expansion as sponsor overhang clears.

items.name: Arm Holdings plc
items.thesis: ARM IPO'd Sep 2023; SoftBank holds ~90%; gradual lockup expirations and Vision Fund repositioning create chronic supply overhang.
items.edge: Periodic secondary distributions can create entry opportunities; AI-CPU royalty story still compelling with strong long-term TAM.

items.name: Travere Therapeutics
items.thesis: Travere FILSPARI sNDA approved April 13, 2026 for FSGS — first and only approved FSGS therapy; major label expansion catalyst.
items.edge: Stock surged 37% on approval; ramp into second rare-disease indication doubles addressable market; FSGS ~40K patients.

items.name: Axsome Therapeutics
items.thesis: AXS-05 PDUFA April 30, 2026 for Alzheimer's disease agitation — first oral therapy in indication; commercial launch in 2H 2026.
items.edge: Profitable revenue base from MDD label provides downside cushion; AD agitation could double addressable market.

items.name: Lantheus Holdings
items.thesis: Lantheus LNTH-2501 (Ga 68 edotreotide) PDUFA extended 3 months to June 29, 2026; SSTR+ NET diagnostic; manufacturing-only review issue.
items.edge: Extension on manufacturing not efficacy reduces binary risk; PNT2003 (Lutathera generic) expected to begin June 2026 post 30-month stay.

items.name: Denali Therapeutics
items.thesis: Denali AVLAYAH (tividenofusp alfa) approved March 25, 2026 for Hunter syndrome under accelerated approval; commercial launch 2026.
items.edge: First-mover in CNS-penetrating Hunter syndrome therapy; ultra-orphan economics; competing with Takeda's Elaprase.

items.name: Replimune Group
items.thesis: Replimune RP1 received second CRL April 10, 2026 for advanced melanoma — stock plunged 90%+; busted FDA binary now potential restart play.
items.edge: Crushed valuation creates cash-floor analysis; pipeline of RP2 (uveal melanoma) and RP3 (HCC) provide optionality at distressed valuation.

items.name: Viking Therapeutics
items.thesis: Viking VK2735 GLP-1/GIP obesity drug Phase 1 maintenance dosing readout mid-2026; competes with LLY/NVO orals.
items.edge: Pure-play obesity biotech with multiple shots on goal; M&A speculation from Big Pharma chasing obesity TAM.

items.name: Ionis Pharmaceuticals
items.thesis: Ionis royalty on Spinraza — high-dose nusinersen FDA-approved March 30, 2026 (PDUFA April 3); royalty stream enhancement.
items.edge: Royalty stream extension on franchise that was facing biosimilar/Evrysdi pressure; recently refinanced 2026 converts.

items.name: TG Therapeutics
items.thesis: TG Therapeutics Briumvi growing in MS; 2026 sBLA filings expected for subcutaneous formulation; profitable inflection in 2026.
items.edge: Profitable on growing Briumvi script base; subcutaneous form could expand share vs Roche's Ocrevus.
catalysts: Subcutaneous Briumvi data and BLA filing

items.name: Kenvue Inc
items.thesis: Kenvue overhang from talc and Tylenol litigation; J&J indemnifies for talc; KMB merger close H2 2026 at ~$18 implied price.
items.edge: Trades $0.60 below implied deal terms; J&J indemnity caps talc exposure; merger arb spread plus litigation resolution dual catalyst.

items.name: Johnson & Johnson
items.thesis: J&J talc litigation entering mediation phase April 2026 with veteran mediator Fouad Kurdi; 66K+ active cases pressing toward global resolution.
items.edge: Removal of major overhang would re-rate JNJ multiple; pharma franchise underpins downside.

items.name: Bayer AG
items.thesis: Bayer $7.25B Roundup class action settlement received preliminary approval March 2026; final approval July 2026 hearing — major overhang lifting.
items.edge: Resolves bulk of remaining 65K Roundup claims; multi-year overhang dissipates; Bayer trades at distressed multiple.

items.name: Vertiv Holdings
items.thesis: Vertiv added to S&P 500 effective March 23, 2026; passive index demand ~$2.5B+ flows; AI data center capex tailwind.
items.edge: Index inclusion buying largely complete but typically supports stock; structural AI infrastructure tailwind continues.

items.name: Lumentum Holdings
items.thesis: Lumentum added to S&P 500 March 23, 2026; Nvidia $2B investment; passive flows + AI optical demand drive momentum.
items.edge: Pure-play AI optical photonics with Nvidia anchor; index demand still digesting.

items.name: Coherent Corp
items.thesis: Coherent added to S&P 500 March 23, 2026; Nvidia $2B investment in optical transceiver capacity; AI infrastructure pure-play.
items.edge: Index buying coupled with Nvidia anchor demand; legacy industrial laser business underpins downside.

items.name: Match Group Inc
items.thesis: Match Group removed from S&P 500 March 23, 2026 — passive forced selling created technical low; activists circling.
items.edge: Forced index selling creates short-term overhang; multiple activists (Anson, Starboard) pushing for change; potential PE take-out.

items.name: Lamb Weston Holdings
items.thesis: Lamb Weston removed from S&P 500 March 23, 2026; passive forced selling combined with weak fries demand creates value entry.
items.edge: Forced selling overhang clearing; cyclical fries demand should recover with QSR traffic stabilization.

items.name: Wix.com Ltd
items.thesis: Wix completed $1.75B modified Dutch auction tender at $92/share April 2026; massive capital return shows confidence; shares jumped 9.9%.
items.edge: $92 tender price established as a reference floor signal from management; remaining float reduced ~30%; stock at $70 below tender price implies ~30% upside to that signal.

items.name: Scholastic Corporation
items.thesis: Scholastic $200M modified Dutch auction tender at $36-$40/share expires April 20, 2026; ~25% of float at floor; insiders not tendering.
items.edge: Tender floor at $36 = potential price floor; if undersubscribed at $40, supports re-rating; insiders not tendering signals confidence.

items.name: Yext Inc
items.thesis: Yext modified Dutch auction tender expired March 18, 2026; reduced from $180M to $140M at $5.75-$6.50; tender price floor near current price.
items.edge: Significant float reduction at tight tender pricing; established price floor; upside if AI search/SEO licensing materializes.

items.name: Nakamoto
items.thesis: Bitcoin treasury company Nakamoto filed proxy April 7, 2026 for 1-for-20 to 1-for-50 reverse split to maintain Nasdaq listing.
items.edge: Highly speculative bitcoin proxy with reverse split overhang; post-split selling pressure typically creates short opportunity, then mean-reversion entry.

items.name: VCI Global Limited
items.thesis: VCI Global executed 1-for-60 reverse stock split February 27, 2026 to maintain Nasdaq listing — extreme split signals distress.
items.edge: Post-split capitulation typical; if business survives, deep distress entry; otherwise zero. Asymmetric small-position trade.

items.name: NextPlat Corp
items.thesis: NextPlat executed 1-for-10 reverse split effective April 13, 2026 for Nasdaq bid-price compliance — typical post-split chop opportunity.
items.edge: Post-split capitulation creates micro-cap entry; exits dollar-trader pressure; but 12-month rule prevents another split if needs more cure.

items.name: MultiSensor AI Holdings
items.thesis: MultiSensor AI executed reverse split mid-April 2026 for Nasdaq compliance; AI/sensor microcap with potential thematic re-rate post-cure.
items.edge: AI thematic exposure on small float post-split; lottery-ticket-style asymmetric microcap setup.

items.name: Cable One Inc
items.thesis: Cable One repaid $575M 0% converts at March 15, 2026 maturity using revolver — overhang now removed but adds revolver utilization risk.
items.edge: Convert overhang removed; equity dilution avoided; but increased secured debt and Moody's downgrade concerns persist.
catalysts: Permanent refi via high-yield bond issuance

items.name: Duke Energy
items.thesis: Duke Energy issued $1B converts March 2026 to refinance $1.725B 4.125% converts maturing April 15, 2026; equity overhang during refi window.
items.edge: Refi mostly digested; resolves looming $1.725B maturity overhang; defensive utility with AI data-center load growth tailwind.

items.name: Etsy Inc
items.thesis: Etsy has $650M 0.125% converts due Sept 2026 deeply out-of-the-money; equity-overhang as company likely refinances via cash/new debt.
items.edge: Refi risk priced in; cash balance and FCF could absorb maturity; potential resolution catalyst removes overhang.

items.name: Twilio Inc
items.thesis: Twilio has $1.0B 3.625% converts due March 2029, $1.0B 3.875% converts due 2031; strong cash position derisks maturity wall.
items.edge: Cash-rich balance sheet means converts can be settled in cash; activists pressuring for buybacks add upside.

items.name: EchoStar Corp
items.thesis: EchoStar joined S&P 500 March 23, 2026 alongside AI-infra trio; spectrum value monetization and AT&T deal underpin re-rating.
items.edge: Index inclusion buying combined with spectrum monetization deleveraging; pure-play special situation.

items.name: Molina Healthcare
items.thesis: Molina removed from S&P 500 March 23, 2026 — passive forced selling alongside Medicaid redetermination overhang creates entry.
items.edge: Forced index selling typically creates 5-10% short-term lows; Medicaid redetermination cycle nearing exhaustion.

items.name: Ciena Corp
items.thesis: Ciena added to S&P 500 February 2026 replacing Dayforce post-acquisition; passive flows + AI optical thematic drove re-rating.
items.edge: Index demand combined with AI optical demand; long-cycle webscale capex tailwind.

items.name: Paycom Software
items.thesis: Paycom removed from S&P 500 March 23, 2026; founder-led HCM platform with high FCF; forced selling creates multi-year value entry.
items.edge: Forced index selling overhang creates technical low; high-FCF founder-led platform with rule-of-40 metrics.
